pages.name: One-Shot Overview
pages.content:
The Golden Hour

An introductory one-shot for the Gilded Stasis campaign. Level 10. ~4.5 hours.

Premise

Set three years before the main campaign, during the 7th Grand Heroic Games. Players are The Silver Chalice, a renowned adventuring party invited to compete. They will meet Valerius Thorne at the height of his public persona, fight alongside the Iron Vanguard at their prime, and experience the golden age that Valerius wants to preserve forever.

The DM Secret

Everything in this one-shot is designed to make Valerius look like the greatest leader the Tri-Kingdoms has ever produced. There are NO red flags, NO sinister moments. He is warm, sincere, and heroic. The horror happens later, in the main campaign, when the players realize the man they loved was freezing heroes in stasis pods.

Five-Act Structure

ActTimeContent
1 — Arrival45 minTravel, meet Elara, Heroes' Rest, Iron Vanguard encounter, Valerius reception
2 — Gauntlet75 min3 Crucible combat waves, Labyrinth puzzles, Champion's Trial boss
3 — Gala30 minRoleplay, Iron Vanguard bonding, Valerius's speech
4 — Rift Crisis75 minTemporal Rift opens, civilian rescue, building collapse, Colossus boss
5 — Golden Hour30 minCeremony, medals, The Offer, farewell, epilogue

pages.name: Connections to Main Campaign
pages.content:
Secret Connections — One-Shot to Main Campaign

These emotional landmines planted by the one-shot detonate during the main campaign.

One-Shot MomentMain Campaign Payoff
Meeting Bron, Torq, Pip, Sylara as vibrant, alive peopleFinding them frozen in stasis pods in the Citadel (Phase 3)
Valerius saving civilians, fighting alongside the PCsDiscovering he is the BBEG who freezes heroes (Phase 2–3)
“You are the best of us” speechHe means it literally — he wants to preserve the "best" (Phase 3)
“A rest you’ve truly earned” / The OfferStasis pods. That's the “rest.” (Phase 3, Citadel)
Valerius's personal letter about “a home”Finding the letter filed next to the PCs' frozen stasis pods (Phase 3)
The Silver Chalice pre-gen charactersFinding those exact characters frozen in the Hall of Statues (Phase 2–3)
Sylara's “Gold twenty. Black one.”She SAW this future — her Portent warned her and she went anyway (Phase 3)
Torq's terrible, wonderful poemFinding a copy in his pod, clutched in his frozen hands (Phase 3)
Pip's handmade silver chalice pinFinding it on her frozen body, or in a display case (Phase 3)
Bron's “See you at the Eighth Games!”He won the 8th Games. Then vanished. (Phase 2, lore discovery)

pages.name: The Golden Invitation
pages.content:
By Order of His Grace, Archduke Valerius Thorne

To the esteemed members of The Silver Chalice —

You are hereby invited to compete in the Seventh Grand Heroic Games in the glorious City of Aurelius.

Your deeds have not gone unnoticed. The people of the Tri-Kingdoms speak your names with gratitude, and it is my honor to welcome you among the finest heroes our realm has ever produced.

The Games will feature:

  • The Gauntlet of Heroes — a three-stage test of combat, wit, and spirit
  • The Grand Victory Gala — a celebration worthy of your accomplishments
  • The Ceremony of the Seventh Star — where champions are crowned

Your lodging at the Heroes' Rest has been arranged. All expenses are provided for by the Crown.

I look forward to greeting you personally.

With deepest admiration,

Archduke Valerius Thorne
Steward of the Grand Heroic Games
Keeper of the Tri-Kingdoms' Legacy

pages.name: Gauntlet of Heroes — Official Rules
pages.content:
THE GAUNTLET OF HEROES — Official Rules

Presented by the Office of the Archduke

STAGE 1 — THE CRUCIBLE

An arena combat trial in three waves.

  • All damage is non-lethal. Arena healers stand ready.
  • Creatures are arena-trained and magically harnessed.
  • Teams may use any spells, abilities, or items at their disposal.
  • Surrender is permitted (no shame, but the crowd will boo).

STAGE 2 — THE LABYRINTH

A test of mind and spirit.

  • Teams navigate a shifting magical maze.
  • Combat is NOT expected (but anything can happen).
  • The Labyrinth responds to teamwork, not brute force.

STAGE 3 — THE CHAMPION'S TRIAL

One final challenge for the glory of the Seventh Star.

  • Face the legendary Golem of the Seventh Star.
  • Victory earns the Medal of the Seventh Star — the highest honor the Games bestow.
  • The Golem fights with honor. It will not strike the fallen.

“The Games celebrate not only strength, but the courage to stand with your comrades when the stakes are highest.”

— Archduke Valerius Thorne, Opening Address, 1st Grand Heroic Games

pages.name: Ode to the Silver Chalice — by Torq
pages.content:
“Ode to the Silver Chalice”

By Torq, Poet Barbarian of the Iron Vanguard

There once was a cup made of silver,
Not gold, not bronze, not a pillar,
They fought through the Gauntlet,
With style and with talent,
And beat up a golem — a killer!

Their healer kept everyone standing,
Their rogue had some moves quite commanding,
The fighter hit stuff,
The ranger was tough,
And the sorcerer — simply outstanding!

So here’s to the Chalice, my friends,
May your glory not meet any ends,
We’ll drink through the night,
Till the morning is bright,
And tomorrow we’ll do it again!

[Handwritten at the bottom in shaky letters:]
“I worked on this for THREE DAYS. — T.”

pages.name: A Personal Note from Valerius
pages.content:
A Personal Note

My dear friends,

I write this not as the Archduke, but as a man who has been moved by what he has seen today.

The Games exist because I believe that heroism is the highest expression of the mortal spirit. That when ordinary people choose to stand against darkness — not because they must, but because they can — the world is made better for it.

You are proof of that belief.

I have seen many champions over seven Games. Fighters who could shatter stone. Mages who could bend the sky. But what you showed today was something rarer — you showed heart. You fought for each other. You protected people who could not protect themselves. You stood when lesser souls would have run.

I want you to know that Aurelius will always have a place for you. When the road grows long and the battles grow weary — when you are ready for a rest you have truly earned — know that you need only return. I will be here.

The world needs heroes. But heroes deserve peace.

With the deepest respect and warmest friendship,

Valerius

[The letter smells faintly of parchment and old books. The handwriting is careful and unhurried — he clearly took time with this.]

name: Player Handout: Medal of the Seventh Star

pages.name: Medal of the Seventh Star
pages.content:
Medal of the Seventh Star

Wondrous Item (Uncommon)

A heavy gold medal in the shape of a seven-pointed star, set with a small sapphire at its center. The names of the Silver Chalice are engraved on the reverse.

Properties

  • While wearing the Medal, you have advantage on Charisma (Persuasion) checks made in the Tri-Kingdoms. People recognize you as a Games champion.
  • Once per long rest, you can invoke the Medal to gain +2 to one attack roll, ability check, or saving throw. You must declare the use before rolling.

“Awarded to the Silver Chalice for valor, cunning, and teamwork in the Seventh Grand Heroic Games.”

Presented by Archduke Valerius Thorne

pages.name: The Victory Gala
pages.content:
That night, Aurelius celebrates.

The Victory Gala spills out of the Archduke's estate into the streets. Enchanted fireworks bloom overhead in shapes of famous heroes — including, to your surprise, silhouettes of each of you recreating your finest moments from the Gauntlet.

Food, music, laughter — the city is alive with it. People stop you in the street to shake your hands. Children ask you to sign their paper swords.

Inside the estate, the competing teams gather. The Iron Vanguard have claimed the biggest table and appear to be three rounds into a drinking contest. Bron stands on his chair the moment he spots you.

"THERE THEY ARE! The Silver Chalice! Get over here!"

pages.name: The Closing Ceremony
pages.content:
The morning after.

The Colosseum is full again — fuller, if that's possible. Word of last night has spread across the city. You're not just Games champions anymore. You saved Aurelius.

Valerius stands on the central platform, looking tired but whole. Beside him, Herald Elara holds five golden medals on a velvet cushion: the Medals of the Seventh Star.

"What happened last night was not part of the Games," Valerius says. "It was real. There was no ward protecting the audience. No handlers waiting in the wings. There was only darkness — and the people who chose to stand against it."

He turns to you.

"The Silver Chalice earned these medals in the Gauntlet. But they earned something greater last night — the gratitude of every soul in this city."

He pins the medals personally, taking a moment with each of you.

pages.name: Farewell at the Gate
pages.content:
At the city gate, the Iron Vanguard waits to see you off.

Bron enfolds each of you in a crushing bear hug. He smells like ale and forge smoke. "We'll see you at the Eighth Games. And next time, the Vanguard's winning!"

Torq hands you a rolled piece of parchment. "A poem. For the road. My best work." Bron mouths "It isn't" behind his back.

Pip appears from nowhere and presses a small pin into the hand of whoever she's most bonded with — a tiny silver chalice, beautifully crafted. "Nicked the materials. Made it myself. Don't tell Bron."

Sylara is last. She takes each of your hands in turn and says nothing for a long time. Then, very quietly:

"I drew the cards last night. For you."

She pauses.

"Gold twenty. Black one."

She doesn't explain. She just smiles — warm, a little sad — and squeezes your hands before letting go.

pages.name: The Gallery of the Rescued
pages.content:
[Read if the players explore the Palace with Valerius or Elara.]

Valerius leads you into a quiet, high-ceilinged wing of the palace, away from the music and the crowd.

Rows of statues line the walls — white marble figures carved with such incredible detail that you can see the texture of their cloaks, the individual links in their mail, and the lines of determination on their faces. They aren't just poses; they are moments. A fighter mid-swing, a cleric in prayer, a rogue leaping from a ledge.

"These are the heroes who gave everything for Aurelius," Valerius says, his voice soft with reverence. "I found a way to... keep them exactly as they were at their finest moment. So we never forget."

He stops before a statue of a young knight and touches the marble hand.

"They deserve to be remembered at their best. Not as broken things, or as dust in a tomb. Here, they are forever heroes. Forever safe. It's the least I could do for them."
